evidences.title: Moldova 0-2 Italy (Nov 13, 2025) Game Analysis - ESPN
evidences.content: Italy remained on the front foot early in the second half, with Andrea Cambiaso seeing his close-range shot blocked by Ioan-Calin Revenco. Scamacca also saw his effort charged down before substitute Pio Esposito tested Kozhukhar with a central volley and then drilled a low shot wide from the edge of the area. Italy, who next face Norway on Sunday, eventually got their reward with just two minutes left when Mancini stooped to glance in a cross from Federico Dimarco. Pio Esposito then headed home a second goal after a deep ball in from Napoli winger Politano on the right to the back post. [...] AS Roma midfielder Bryan Cristante then saw his backheeled flick from a corner pushed around the post by Moldova goalkeeper Andriy Kozhukhar. Riccardo Orsolini sent his 20-yard effort over as Italy continued to keep the home side under pressure. Bologna forward Orsolini was involved again in the 25th minute when his free kick was only half punched clear and defender Mancini fired the loose ball over. Italy players celebrate after scoring a goal against Moldova in World Cup qualifying. Moldova finally threatened when Mihail Stefan got away on a swift counter-attack and his cross was volleyed...

evidences.content: Mateo Retegui and Francesco Pio Esposito came off the bench, immediately seeing efforts charged down from close range, the Inter striker also forcing a save with his scuffed volley from 10 yards on a Matteo Politano cross. Gianluca Scamacca playing for Italy in Moldova during a 2026 World Cup qualifier (@azzurri) Moments later, Esposito swept wide of the near post from Zaccagni’s assist with the inside of the right boot. The mad scramble and series of crosses finally paid off for Italy at the 88th minute with a diving Gianluca Mancini header on Federico Dimarco’s cross from the left, finding the near bottom corner from just inside the area. It was very nearly 2-0 moments later, but Davide Frattesi ballooned over from eight yards on a Retegui pull-back from the by-line. evidences.content: Elsewhere, Alessandro Buongiorno and Samuele Ricci both return; Cagliari goalkeeper Elia Caprile has excelled in Serie A and is Gattuso's sole first-time call-up. Meanwhile, Moldova's main absentee will be versatile midfielder Nichita Motpan, who is currently injured. Italy-based playmaker Artur Ionita returns to the squad after missing out last month, and all-time top scorer Ion Nicolaescu - who averages one goal every three international games - is back from an injury. Moldova possible starting lineup: Avram; Forov, Craciun, Baboglo, Platica, Reabciuk; Caimacov, Rata, Bogaciuc; Postolachi; Nicolaescu Italy possible starting lineup: Donnarumma; Di Lorenzo, Mancini, Bastoni, Dimarco; Cristante, Locatelli, Tonali; Orsolini, Retegui, Zaccagni [...] With his focus already shifting towards a possible playoff semi-final next spring, Italy coach Gattuso has suggested he will field a less familiar XI against Moldova. One regular starter will definitely be omitted, as Nicolo Barella must serve a one-match ban; Roma's in-form midfielder Bryan Cristante could replace him. In attack, Fiorentina striker Moise Kean has been forced to withdraw due to injury, so Bologna winger Nicolo Cambiaghi...

evidences.title: World Cup qualifiers: Kean to miss games against Moldova and ...
evidences.content: Kean out, Cambiaghi in. Gennaro Gattuso has been forced to make this change for the national team’s final two Worl Cup qualifying matches, against Moldova (November 13, Chisinau) and Norway (November 16, Milan). The Fiorentina striker, who picked up an injury while playing for his club, will be unable to join the squad. In his place, the head coach has called up Bologna forward Nicolò Cambiaghi, who made his national team debut on October 14 in Udine in the final minutes of the match against Israel. The Azzurri will meet tomorrow at the National Training Centre in Coverciano, where they will hold their first training session in the afternoon. The camp will officially open at 14:00 CET with Gennaro Gattuso’s press conference. SQUAD LIST [...] FORWARDS: Nicolò Cambiaghi (Bologna),Francesco Pio Esposito (Inter), Riccardo Orsolini (Bologna), Matteo Politano (Napoli), Giacomo Raspadori (Atletico Madrid), Mateo Retegui (Al-Qadsiah), Gianluca Scamacca (Atalanta), Mattia Zaccagni (Lazio). evidences.title: World Cup Qualifiers: 27 players called up for the matches ...
evidences.content: Coach Gennaro Gattuso has called up 27 players for the training camp that starts on Monday, 10 November, at the Coverciano National Training Centre. Cagliari goalkeeper Elia Caprile has been called up for the first time to the senior national team, while Alessandro Buongiorno and Samuele Ricci return to the Azzurri squad, after last representing Italy in March 2025 and June 2025, respectively. Gianluca Scamacca is also able to join the squad again. He had been previously called up by Gattuso in September but had to leave the camp before the match against Estonia in Bergamo because he hadn’t fully recovered from a muscular issue that he had come into Coverciano with. Nicolò Barella, who was booked on 14 October in the game against Israel, will miss the match against Moldova due to [...] In the capital of Lombardy, the Azzurri kept an almost-century-long unbeaten run, lasting exactly 96 years: from the 1-2 loss to Hungary in January 1925 (at Campo Milan, while the other loss to Hungary in 1911 came at Arena Civica) to the 1-2 defeat against Spain in October 2021 at the ‘Meazza’. Italy’s most prominent victory in Milan was the 9-4 win over France on 18 January 1920, while Austria i...

evidences.title: Italy Trembles: Gattuso's Squad Suffers Two Major Blows Before ...
evidences.content: ## ### Retegui and Raspadori to lead the attack With both forwards ruled out, Gattuso is expected to rely on Mateo Retegui (from Al-Qadsiah) and Giacomo Raspadori (of Atlético de Madrid) to lead the offensive line. The duo will be tasked with revitalizing an attack that has struggled to find consistency in recent matches. Italy, absent from the World Cup since Brazil 2014, finds itself in a delicate position. While the Azzurri are guaranteed at least a playoff spot, they still have a slim chance to qualify directly — provided they win their final two matches, overturn a large goal difference, and hope for a Norway slip-up. Their first test comes on November 13 in Chisinau, against Moldova, followed by a decisive clash at the San Siro Stadium in Milan against Erling Haaland’s Norway. evidences.title: One new Italy doubt in attack after Monday's training session
evidences.content: Gattuso confirmed a squad of 27 players to take on Moldova and Norway last week. He has already had to make one alteration to his selections, with Nicolo Cambiaghi coming in to replace the injured Moise Kean, who has dropped out of the squad. ## Raspadori doubt for Italy after Monday’s training session The available players took part in their first training session of the November international break on Monday. Only 12 players (two goalkeepers and 10 outfielders) took part in the training session, while the rest of the squad, most of whom started on Sunday, carried out recovery work in the gym. [...] ...
